meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no coding experience (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no coding experience. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kit for No-Code Builders
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with no-code limits. Use this SaaS starter kit checklist with Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional web apps at lightning speed.
content.answer_block: Overcome no-code limitations by adopting a modular SaaS starter kit. By combining Antigravity’s rapid scaffolding with Claude Code’s autonomous engineering, you transform from a non-technical freelancer into a high-velocity product architect, shipping production-ready, scalable SaaS applications without writing manual boilerplate or struggling with complex architectural constraints.
content.intro:
The cold reality of the freelance grind is that custom development is often gated by a wall of syntax and architectural complexity. You have the vision to solve client problems, but the manual effort to set up authentication, database schemas, and API routes feels like an insurmountable mountain that kills your momentum.

But the game has changed. By leveraging an opinionated SaaS starter kit, you bypass the 'blank page' syndrome entirely. We are replacing slow, manual configuration with an autonomous workflow that treats your intent as the primary code generator, allowing you to ship enterprise-grade software at the speed of thought.

steps.title: Define the SaaS Blueprint
steps.description: Establish your database schema and core features. This step ensures your kit is built on a solid foundation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a SaaS starter kit architecture with Next.js, Tailwind, and Supabase. Include user auth, Stripe checkout, and a protected dashboard route.

steps.title: Automate Database Migrations
steps.description: Use AI to write your Prisma or Drizzle schema based on your app requirements.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a database schema for a project management SaaS including tables for Organizations, Users, and Projects with proper foreign key relationships.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "generate prisma schema from the project requirements.md"

steps.title: Configure Authentication Flow
steps.description: Implement secure authentication quickly so you can focus on the business logic that pays the bills.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement Supabase Auth with Google OAuth and email magic link support within the existing Next.js app structure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "setup supabase auth middleware and protected routes"

steps.title: Integrate Payment Gateways
steps.description: Connect Stripe to handle subscriptions and ensure your SaaS is monetized from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe webhook handler to sync subscription status with the user database table.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "integrate stripe webhooks for subscription lifecycle management"

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Automate your deployment pipeline to ensure your client's project goes live with zero downtime.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a Vercel deployment pipeline with environment variables and build optimization for a production SaaS.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "configure vercel.json and CI/CD pipeline for production release"

pro_tips: Always use a 'Context File' (context.md) in your root to keep Claude Code aligned with your project's specific architectural preferences.
Prioritize 'Feature Flags' early; it allows you to ship broken features to production without breaking the UX for existing clients.
Use Antigravity to generate unit tests for your API routes before you even write the business logic to ensure TDD-like reliability.
Treat your starter kit as a 'Living Repository'—every time you build a new project, commit your improvements back to your base template.
When Claude Code gets stuck, ask it to 'explain the current dependency tree' to identify conflicts before they crash your build.
content.next_steps: Once your first project is deployed, iterate on your starter kit by adding a custom UI component library to ensure your future client projects look unique and professional.
content.conversion_block: Stop building from scratch and start shipping like a pro. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
